item.title: Multi-instance support — deploy multiple agents on the same host with isolated ports, networks, and data dirs
item.intent: A single host must support multiple independent agents without conflicts. Port collisions, shared networks, or overlapping data dirs are deployment failures that must be prevented by construction.
item.description: ClawHQ currently assumes a single agent per host. Deploying a second agent on the same server (different user, different blueprint) will collide on ports, Docker network names, data directories, and image tags. This blocks the primary use case of running agents for multiple people on one powerful machine. Required: (1) per-agent deployment directory (~/.clawhq/<agent-name>/), (2) per-agent Docker network with unique name, (3) per-agent port allocation with collision detection in preflight, (4) per-agent image tags (openclaw:<agent-name>), (5) per-agent container names.
steps: Add instanceName?: string to WizardAnswers (src/design/configure/types.ts), BuildOptions (src/build/docker/types.ts), and ClawHQConfig (src/config/types.ts) — optional field, defaults to 'default' for backward compatibility

Add helper functions to src/config/defaults.ts: agentNetworkName(instanceName?) returns 'clawhq_net' for default, 'clawhq_{name}_net' otherwise; agentImageTag(instanceName?) returns 'openclaw:custom' for default, 'openclaw:{name}' otherwise

Update src/design/configure/generate.ts: replace const AGENT_NETWORK = 'clawhq_net' with call to agentNetworkName(answers.instanceName); thread instanceName into buildClawHQConfig(); default deployDir to ~/.clawhq/{name}/ when name is not 'default'
Update src/build/docker/compose.ts: add networkName parameter (default 'clawhq_net') to generateCompose(); replace two hardcoded 'clawhq_net' references with the parameter

Update src/build/docker/build.ts: replace constant STAGE2_TAG = 'openclaw:custom' with agentImageTag(options.instanceName); STAGE1_TAG stays 'openclaw:local' (shared base image); thread networkName to generateCompose(); update serializeYaml to handle dynamic network name

Fix src/build/launcher/preflight.ts: delete hardcoded const GATEWAY_PORT = GATEWAY_DEFAULT_PORT (line 22); add gatewayPort parameter to runPreflight(); pass through to checkPorts() — falls back to GATEWAY_DEFAULT_PORT when not provided
Update src/build/launcher/deploy.ts: thread gatewayPort from DeployOptions to runPreflight()

Add tests: build with instanceName 'john' produces image tag 'openclaw:john' and network 'clawhq_john_net'; preflight with custom port checks that port; generateBundle with instanceName produces correct network in compose config; two different instance names produce non-colliding artifacts
acceptanceCriteria: Two agents can be deployed on the same host with different names, ports, and data directories
clawhq init with instanceName 'john' creates ~/.clawhq/john/ with isolated config
Docker networks and image tags are unique per agent name — network: clawhq_{name}_net, image: openclaw:{name}
Preflight checks the configured gateway port, not the hardcoded default 18789
Omitting instanceName or using 'default' produces identical artifacts to current behavior (backward compatible)
Stage 1 Docker image (openclaw:local) is shared across all instances — only stage 2 is per-instance
